mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration, measured by quarter-over-quarter changes in GDPC1, signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion. This drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60-day horizon as equity valuations expand in response to improved economic momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2004 real GDP growth reflects post-election economic expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative SPX returns in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 2.16% remains below the estimated neutral rate, indicating accommodative monetary policy. Sustained low rates compress equity risk premiums and support equity valuations. This mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5%.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 monthly additions signals robust labor market demand and consumer spending capacity. Strong employment growth sustains corporate revenue growth and equity valuations over a 5-day horizon as market participants react to labor strength signals.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132,266 thousand as of December 2004
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following PAYEMS > 150k additions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) reflects capacity utilization and manufacturing strength. When INDPRO growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month, it signals supply-side expansion that supports profit margins and equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following INDPRO > 0.3% monthly growth are not significantly positive (mean return not > 0.5%, t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both RSAFS growth exceeds 0.4% month-over-month AND HOUST exceeds 1,700 thousand units annualized, the dual confirmation of demand momentum predicts positive 20-day SPX returns. This interaction captures broad-based demand acceleration.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth > 0.4% AND HOUST > 1,700k units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 349,436 million and HOUST at 1,771 thousand as of December 2004
failure_condition: Dual-trigger instances produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of cases, or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) interact to signal labor market tightness. When UNRATE falls below 5.3% while PAYEMS growth remains positive, labor scarcity emerges, supporting wage growth and consumer spending. This interaction predicts positive 60-day SPX returns in tight labor regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% and PAYEMS at 132,266 thousand as of December 2004
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage pressure
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the joint signal is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate interact to determine real monetary conditions. When CPI growth exceeds 2.5% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5%, real rates turn negative, signaling monetary accommodation. This regime predicts negative 20-day SPX returns as inflation erodes real returns and signals policy error.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 191.2 and FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of December 2004
regime_hint: negative real rates with rising inflation
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns in negative real rate regimes are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the real rate interaction is above -0.04 (i.e., not significantly negative).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) in a regime of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% annual growth) signals stagflationary pressure. Supply constraints combined with price acceleration reduce real corporate profitability and equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 60-day SPX returns when supply tightness meets inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 117.813 and CPIAUCSL at 191.2 as of December 2004
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns in stagflationary regimes are positive in more than 48% of instances, or Rank IC of the stagflation interaction is above -0.05 (not significantly negative).
